name: Planet Name
description: Planet name most commonly used in the literature

name: Host Name
description: Stellar name most commonly used in the literature

name: Default Parameter Set
description: Boolean flag indicating whether given set of planet parameters has been selected as default (1=yes, 0=no)

name: Number of Stars
description: Number of gravitationally bound stars in the planetary system

name: Number of Planets
description: Number of confirmed planets in the planetary system

name: Number of Moons
description: Number of moons in the planetary system

name: Circumbinary Flag
description: Flag indicating whether the planet orbits a binary system (1=yes, 0=no)

name: Discovery Year
description: Year the planet was discovered

name: Controversial Flag
description: Flag indicating whether the confirmation status of a planet has been questioned in the published literature (1=yes, 0=no)

name: Orbital Period [days]
description: Time the planet takes to make a complete orbit around the host star or system

name: Orbit Semi-Major Axis [au]
description: The longest radius of an elliptic orbit, or, for exoplanets detected via gravitational microlensing or direct imaging, the projected separation in the plane of the sky

name: Planet Radius [Earth Radius]
description: Length of a line segment from the center of the planet to its surface, measured in units of radius of the Earth

name: Planet Radius [Jupiter Radius]
description: Length of a line segment from the center of the planet to its surface, measured in units of radius of Jupiter

name: Planet Mass or Mass*sin(i) [Earth Mass]
description: Best planet mass estimate available, in order of preference: Mass, M*sin(i)/sin(i), or M*sin(i), depending on availability, and measured in Earth masses

name: Planet Mass or Mass*sin(i) [Jupiter Mass]
description: Best planet mass estimate available, in order of preference: Mass, M*sin(i)/sin(i), or M*sin(i), depending on availability, and measured in Jupiter masses

name: Planet Mass or Mass*sin(i) Provenance
description: Provenance of the measurement of the best mass. Options are: Mass, M*sin(i)/sin(i), and M*sini

name: Insolation Flux [Earth Flux]
description: Insolation flux is another way to give the equilibrium temperature. It's given in units relative to those measured for the Earth from the Sun.

name: Equilibrium Temperature [K]
description: The equilibrium temperature of the planet as modeled by a black body heated only by its host star, or for directly imaged planets, the effective temperature of the planet required to match the measured luminosity if the planet were a black body

name: Stellar Effective Temperature [K]
description: Temperature of the star as modeled by a black body emitting the same total amount of electromagnetic radiation

name: Stellar Radius [Solar Radius]
description: Length of a line segment from the center of the star to its surface, measured in units of radius of the Sun

name: Stellar Mass [Solar mass]
description: Amount of matter contained in the star, measured in units of masses of the Sun

name: Stellar Surface Gravity [log10(cm/s**2)]
description: Gravitational acceleration experienced at the stellar surface

name: Distance [pc]
description: Distance to the planetary system in units of parsecs
